subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF Art IV, World Bank, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, Mo Ibrahim) + international press.

A stable democracy (the March 2025 inauguration of Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah, its first female president, capped a clean cycle) and an African press-freedom leader, with a credible but time-distant oil/green-hydrogen upside.

Standout features: strong institutions and a transformational Orange-Basin oil/hydrogen optionality (first oil ~2029-30, unrealized), against among the world's worst inequality (~36% unemployment), a rand-peg, and severe drought. Symmetric with a positive long-tail.

economic.notes: Steady but rand-constrained and mineral-dependent, with a large unrealized oil/hydrogen upside.

fiscal_state.notes: Manageable but constrained - moderate deficits/debt, with the IMF urging consolidation amid drought and social spending. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: The Namibian dollar is pegged 1:1 to the rand in the Common Monetary Area, effectively importing South African policy. (Bank of Namibia)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The Namibian dollar is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and easing (~3.5% in 2025 toward ~2.9%), anchored by the rand peg. (Bank of Namibia)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised and supervised, with reserves adequate to sustain the peg. (Bank of Namibia)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Mineral-concentrated (uranium, diamonds) and dented by weak diamond demand, with offshore oil/green-hydrogen potentially diversifying exports later this decade. (AfDB; IMF)

institutional.notes: Independent courts, credible data, and a world-leading press environment.

rule_of_law.notes: An independent judiciary and broadly respected rule of law, among Africa's better performers. (Freedom House 2025; Mo Ibrahim)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Reasonably capable by regional standards, stretched by high unemployment and delivery demands. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: A regional and global leader - RSF ranked Namibia near the world's top in 2025. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Stable democracy and protected liberties, against among the world's worst inequality.

political_stability.notes: Stable, with an orderly 2024-25 cycle and SWAPO continuity under a new president. (Al Jazeera; ECN)

civil_liberties.notes: Broadly protected - rated 'Free' with vibrant civil society and press. (Freedom House 2025)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Generally tolerant, with legacy marginalisation issues and an unresolved colonial-genocide reparations dynamic. (HRW)

social_cohesion.notes: Politically stable but among the world's most unequal (Gini ~0.6) with ~36% unemployment. (World Bank)

demographics.notes: A young potential dividend blunted by ~17% HIV and very high youth unemployment. (AfDB; UNAIDS)

geopolitical.notes: A peaceful, non-aligned, sanction-free partner integrated with South Africa.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable non-aligned partner tied to South Africa (CMA/SACU), courting diversified energy investment. (observed)

conflict_involvement.notes: No armed conflict, peaceful internally and externally. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No sanctions exposure, broadly open under the CMA. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Good regional infrastructure, against extreme aridity and drought.

climate.notes: Among SSA's driest; a severe 2023-24 El Nino drought emergency with chronic water stress. (UN OCHA)

healthcare.notes: Functional but burdened by high HIV prevalence and rural gaps. (WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Comparatively good - Walvis Bay port, roads, and growing energy investment. (World Bank; AfDB)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate; safer than several neighbours, with urban property/violent crime. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: High drought exposure with limited penetration. (inference)

summary: A stable, well-governed democracy - the March 2025 inauguration of Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah (its first female president, SWAPO continuity) capped a clean electoral cycle, and the country is an African leader on press freedom. The macro picture is steady but constrained: the rand peg caps monetary independence, growth is mineral-dependent and was dented by weak diamond demand and a severe 2024 drought, and the binding problem is among the world's worst inequality with ~36% unemployment. The major swing factor is Orange-Basin oil and green-hydrogen optionality - potentially transformational but time-distant (first oil ~2029-30) and not yet de-risked - leaving a symmetric skew with a positive long-tail.
